description: Address review findings: add thread safety mutex, fix JSON parser update boundary bug, replace raw MKTelegram* with std::unique_ptr, and route Telegram natural language messages through the AI pipeline.
steps: Add #include <mutex> and add a std::mutex member to MKSystem for shared state protection
Wrap Telegram polling thread calls to MKSystem methods in lock_guard
Wrap REPL thread mutations (handle_natural_query, cmd_learn, etc.) in lock_guard
Fix JSON parser in telegram_poll_loop to bound field searches to the next update_id position
Route natural language Telegram messages through the AI pipeline (simplified handle_natural_query)
acceptance_criteria: Telegram polling thread acquires lock before calling any MKSystem methods
REPL thread acquires lock when mutating graph/memory state
JSON parser bounds chat_id and text searches to stop at next update_id position
MKTelegram is managed via std::unique_ptr instead of raw pointer
Natural language Telegram messages are routed through AI pipeline, not just echoed
findings: Build passes with 11 pre-existing warnings. All 7 tests pass (62 assertions). The generate_ai_response function provides a simplified AI pipeline for Telegram that uses the router, graph, vector search, and composer without the full REPL display logic. The mutex uses a coarse-grained single-lock approach which is sufficient for this use case since the critical sections are short-lived.
